      <title>Bound by blood: new film highlights Jamaica’s outlawed obeah belief system</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/30/stew-pews-film-obeah-jamaica-magic-tradition</link>
      <description><p>Stew Peas focuses on obeah, an enduring African magic practice in Jamaica banned by colonisers in the 1700s</p><p>A new movie from award-winning Jamaican film-maker Sosiessia Nixon shines a spotlight on Jamaica’s enduring west African-based magic and spiritual healing tradition known as obeah.</p><p>Nixon’s tense, feature-length suspense, <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=42iXg3kn490">Stew Peas</a>, tells of the story of Jamaican detective Tessa, who is obsessed with an old murder case.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/30/stew-pews-film-obeah-jamaica-magic-tradition">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>WHO puts Ebola outbreak death rate at ‘huge’ 30-50% as chief arrives in DRC</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/29/who-chief-tedros-adhanom-ghebreyesus-drc-ebola-outbreak-epidemic</link>
      <description><p>Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus calls for ceasefire among armed groups to help avoid deaths from preventable disease</p><p>The death rate of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is between 30% and 50%, the World Health Organization has said, as its head arrived in the country to support efforts to contain the disease.</p><p>Anaïs Legand, from the WHO’s high threat pathogens team, said the revised death rate estimate is based on confirmed cases. “It’s huge. It means that up to five out of 10 people are likely to die,” Legand told reporters in Geneva.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/29/who-chief-tedros-adhanom-ghebreyesus-drc-ebola-outbreak-epidemic">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>Friday briefing: ​What do the cuts in aid mean for the fight against Ebola in the DRC?</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/29/friday-briefing-what-do-the-cuts-in-aid-mean-for-the-fight-against-ebola-in-the-drc</link>
      <description><p>In today’s newsletter: As the virus spreads across borders, health workers warn that weakened global support is making a prolonged crisis more likely</p><p></p><p>Ebola is spreading rapidly in parts of east Africa. The deadly disease, which kills around half of those it infects, is <a href="https://www.who.int/emergencies/alert-and-response">suspected to have claimed</a> the lives of at least 240 people since the outbreak began in Ituri province in the Democratic Republic of the Congo earlier this month.</p><p>Public health officials are scrambling to contain the virus in one of the toughest environments: Ituri province, the centre of the crisis<strong>,</strong> is a mining hub where thousands of people work in close proximity every day, and a conflict zone, with ongoing fighting between rebel groups. Medical facilities are modest, while waves of displaced people are being forced into overcrowded camps to escape fighting<strong>, </strong>making it even harder to control transmission. The virus has already spread to other regions in eastern DRC and the Ugandan capital Kampala.</p><p><em><strong>UK news </strong></em>| Britain risks <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/business/2026/may/28/uk-risks-125bn-hit-youth-unemployment-landmark-report-alan-milburn-neets">a financial hit worth £125bn a year</a> after a rise in the number of young people not in employment or education to more than 1 million.</p><p><em><strong>US-Israel-Iran </strong></em>| Donald Trump has circulated <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/28/donald-trump-shares-draft-iran-peace-agreement-with-israel-and-other-allies">a draft peace agreement</a> for the war with Iran among allies including Israel as both sides try to prevent fresh breaches of the ceasefire escalating out of control.</p><p><em><strong>UK politics </strong></em>| Andy Burnham has <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2026/may/28/andy-burnham-immigration-benefits-policy-makerfield-labour">rolled back</a> from his previous calls for ministers to scrap a restriction on immigrants claiming benefits as the Makerfield byelection places greater scrutiny on him.</p><p><em><strong>Ukraine </strong></em>| A Russian drone that was part of an overnight attack on Ukraine crashed into an apartment building <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/29/russian-drone-romanian-apartments-wounding-two-people-and-starting-fire">in eastern Romania</a>, injuring two people, authorities said, in what an official statement condemned as an “irresponsible escalation” by Moscow.</p><p><em><strong>Climate crisis </strong></em>| Abandoning net zero and drilling for more oil and gas would be a <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/environment/2026/may/28/tony-blair-fossil-fuel-advice-bizarre-energy-climate-crises-experts-say">massive setback for the UK</a> and would not help the economy, leading experts have said in response to Tony Blair.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/29/friday-briefing-what-do-the-cuts-in-aid-mean-for-the-fight-against-ebola-in-the-drc">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>Dormitory fire at Kenyan girls’ school kills at least 16 students</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/28/dormitory-fire-at-kenya-school-students-utumishi-girls-academy-gilgil</link>
      <description><p>Parents face anxious wait for updates after blaze tears through Utumishi girls academy in Gilgil, Nakuru county</p><p>A fire has ripped through a dormitory at a girls’ school in Kenya’s Rift valley, killing at least 16 students.</p><p>The fire broke out just after midnight at Utumishi girls academy in Gilgil, Nakuru county, about 76 miles north-west of Nairobi, police said.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/28/dormitory-fire-at-kenya-school-students-utumishi-girls-academy-gilgil">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>US building Ebola quarantine center in Kenya for Americans amid outbreak</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/may/27/trump-administration-ebola-quarantine-kenya</link>
      <description><p>Some experts criticize White House approach and say not allowing Americans to return to US hurts treatment efforts</p><p>The <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/trump-administration">Trump administration</a> is building a quarantine and treatment center in Kenya for Americans affected by the <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/ebola">Ebola</a> outbreak, instead of bringing them home.</p><p>The White House on Wednesday confirmed that the US was setting up a facility in Kenya for Americans to quarantine after Ebola exposure in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC).</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/may/27/trump-administration-ebola-quarantine-kenya">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>Colombia goes to polls in election pitting outgoing leader’s ally against pro-Trump candidates</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/31/colombia-polls-election-outgoing-leaders-ally-pro-trump-candidates</link>
      <description><p>Ballots are being cast in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential elections</p><p>Colombians are casting ballots in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential election, choosing between candidates with radically diverging visions for the future of peace in a country haunted by decades of armed conflict.</p><p>The vote on Sunday, seen as a referendum on outgoing President Gustavo Petro’s policies, comes 10 years after Colombia signed a historic peace pact with guerrillas of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc).</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/31/colombia-polls-election-outgoing-leaders-ally-pro-trump-candidates">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>Colombia prepares to go to polls in election shadowed by resurgence of political violence</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/30/colombia-presidential-election-vote-resurgence-political-violence</link>
      <description><p>Sunday’s presidential vote is contest between left and right – and between contradictory proposals for dealing with the decades-long armed conflict</p><p>Mateo Pérez Rueda was one internship away from completing a degree in political science. The 24-year-old also worked as a bicycle delivery rider and sold fruit salads and juice to finance his passion: the Colombian independent digital magazine <a href="https://www.instagram.com/el_confidente_yarumal/">El Confidente</a>.</p><p>On 4 May he travelled to Briceño, in the western province of Antioquia, to report on the long-running conflict between the army, paramilitaries and dissidents of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc).</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/30/colombia-presidential-election-vote-resurgence-political-violence">Continue reading...</a></description>

      </media:content>
      <media:content width="700" url="https://i.guim.co.uk/img/media/c1579ea8824fb84ec2c453a6b0b76108341221aa/281_0_5839_4672/master/5839.jpg?width=700&quality=85&auto=format&fit=max&s=4db1bb40d80722f3f17d7e8a8e5d10f1">

      <description><p>Opposition says constitutional amendment would give bill ruling party carte blanche to overturn will of voters</p><ul><li><p><a href="https://www.theguardian.com/news/2026/feb/17/sign-up-for-the-breaking-news-us-email-to-get-newsletter-alerts-direct-to-your-inbox?utm_medium=ACQUISITIONS_STANDFIRST&amp;utm_campaign=BN22326&amp;utm_content=signup&amp;utm_term=standfirst&amp;utm_source=GUARDIAN_WEB">Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email</a></p></li></ul><p>Amid fierce criticism from opposition groups, <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/mexico">Mexico</a>’s senate has passed ‌a constitutional amendment to include “foreign interference” as grounds to annul election results in the country.</p><p>The bill, which was presented by the country’s president, Claudia Sheinbaum, defines foreign interference as “illicit financing, propaganda, the systematic ⁠dissemination of misinformation, digital manipulation, and ⁠the intervention of foreign governments ⁠or agencies”.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/29/mexico-senate-election-constitutional-amendment">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/29/brazil-gangs-terrorist-rubio</link>
      <description><p>Marco Rubio made announcement after meeting president’s far-right challenger Flávio Bolsonaro</p><p>Brazil will not be treated as a “tinpot country,” the country’s president, Luiz Inácio da Silva, said on Friday after the United States designated Brazil’s two largest criminal gangs, the First Capital Command (PCC) and the Red Command, as foreign terrorist organisations.</p><p>The announcement, made by Marco Rubio, the secretary of state, on Thursday, is being widely seen in Brazil as a setback for Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, the president who had strongly opposed the designation – and a boost for Lula’s main challenger in October’s presidential election, the far-right senator Flávio Bolsonaro.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/29/brazil-gangs-terrorist-rubio">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/may/30/moana-pasifika-sign-off-super-rugby-pacific-hymn-win</link>
      <description><ul><li><p>Undermanned side upsets finals-bound ACT Brumbies 21-19</p></li><li><p>Players come together to mark victory and club’s expected demise</p></li></ul><p>An undermanned Moana Pasifika have capped off their potential final match with a stirring victory, upsetting the finals-bound ACT Brumbies 21-19.</p><p>But there were mixed feelings as players celebrated a rare win before coming together with staff to mark the occasion of the club’s farewell game and expected demise with an emotionally charged hymn.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/may/30/moana-pasifika-sign-off-super-rugby-pacific-hymn-win">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>This blog is now closed</p><ul><li><p>Get our <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/email-newsletters?CMP=cvau_sfl">breaking news email</a>, <a href="https://app.adjust.com/w4u7jx3">free app</a> or <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/australia-news/series/full-story?CMP=cvau_sfl">daily news podcast</a></p></li></ul><p><strong>Extra negative gearing limits could hurt market and family budgets, Labor says</strong></p><p><strong>Clare O’Neil</strong> has rejected calls from the Greens and others to put further limits on negative gearing access, saying the government should not interrupt “immediate arrangements”.</p><p>There’s people in the debate who want to see the government go further. We’ll do that in the appropriate time given the need … This is not a political timeline. It is a policy timeline … It’s important this gets resolved speedily and that’s what the government is working towards.</p><p>I think there’s a range of things that are on the table in those conversations and I won’t speak about them in detail …</p><p>The government wants to get the right outcome here and we are not going to be driven by the politics of the moment.       <title>The household battery revolution that could change energy bills … and the world</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/environment/ng-interactive/2026/may/31/cheaper-energy-bills-battery-revolution-climate-crisis</link>
      <description><p>Australia is pioneering a revolution in home renewables and battery use, proving what is possible with the right policies</p><p>The timing was rich with symbolism. As intense heatwaves pummelled Europe and Asia, and oil markets around the world leapt and sputtered, the two big chimneys of one of Australia’s largest power stations were being demolished. Meanwhile, the Australian energy minister was holding a media conference to hail a <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/australia-news/2026/may/26/power-bills-drop-fall-australia-energy-default-market-offer">fall of up to 10% in the benchmark electricity price</a> in parts of the country.</p><p>Quietly, and with surprisingly little fanfare from the rest of the world, Australia is pioneering a revolution in home renewables and battery use, proving what is possible with the right policies. The country was already one of the global leaders in domestic solar power, with <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/environment/2023/nov/01/how-generous-subsidies-helped-australia-to-become-a-leader-in-solar-power">panels on one in three</a> homes. It also remains, however, a major contributor to the climate crisis through its vast fossil fuel exports.<strong> </strong>But it is batteries that are giving Australia a new burst of speed.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/environment/ng-interactive/2026/may/31/cheaper-energy-bills-battery-revolution-climate-crisis">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>There is hope that a change to building regulations could resurrect music clubs, which have been hit by rising rents, social shifts and noise disputes</p><p>A move by the German government to reclassify nightclubs to distinguish them from amusement and adult entertainment facilities could give a much-needed boost to the country’s struggling nightlife, industry advocates say.</p><p>Under a fundamental change to building regulations approved by Friedrich Merz’s cabinet last week, nightclubs will be formally recognised as providing cultural and artistic value, making it more difficult for developers to evict venue operators in favour of new construction.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/31/germany-nightlife-scene-reclassify-clubs-berlin">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Reggio Emilia prefect stops gig after Jewish community ‘concerns’ over rapper’s previous antisemitic remarks</p><p>A Kanye West concert in Italy has been cancelled over “public order and safety issues”.</p><p>The 48-year-old rapper, who changed his name to Ye in 2021, was due to perform at the Pulse of Gaia festival at the RCF Arena in Reggio Emilia on 18 July, but the city’s prefect, Salvatore Angieri, stopped the gigs after “concerns” from the local Jewish community over previous antisemitic remarks by West.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/music/2026/may/30/kanye-west-concert-italy-cancelled-public-order-and-safety-issues">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Experts say AI firm’s engagement with Vatican risks creating ‘feelgood’ discourse that lacks critical examination</p><p>Why did Anthropic’s founder sit beside the pope during a warning about AI?</p><p>In the first major written teaching of his papacy, Pope Leo XIV took artificial intelligence to task.       <description><p>Defence minister announces seizure of fortress as advance against Hezbollah moves beyond Litani River</p><ul><li><p><a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/live/2026/may/31/middle-east-crisis-live-israel-lebanon-beaufort-castle-us-iran-nuclear-deal-hormuz-oil-latest-news-updates">Middle East crisis – live updates</a></p></li></ul><p>Israeli troops have captured the 900-year-old Beaufort Castle and its strategic ridge in southern Lebanon in a significant advance against Hezbollah that took them beyond the Litani River – their deepest incursion into the country in more than 26 years.</p><p>After days of intense fighting and airstrikes in nearby villages, the Israeli defence minister, Israel Katz, said the military had captured the fortress, also known as Qalaat al-Shaqif, which the Israel Defense Forces used as a base during their previous occupation of southern Lebanon between 1982 and 2000.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/31/israel-pursuing-scorced-earth-policy-says-lebanon-pm-as-more-airstrikes-hit-countrys-south">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Senior lawyers call on prime minister to request Indian prosecutors drop charges that would breach double jeopardy rule</p><p>Four senior lawyers, including the former attorney general Dominic Grieve, have written to Keir Starmer urging him to request that Indian prosecutors <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2025/oct/01/keir-starmer-narendra-modi-india-jagtar-singh-johal-release">drop charges against the British national Jagtar Singh Johal</a> on the basis that continued prosecution would be in manifest breach of the double jeopardy rule which prevents someone being tried twice for the same offence.</p><p>Johal has been held in an Indian jail for eight years, and in March last year was <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2025/mar/04/british-sikh-activist-jagtar-singh-johal-acquitted-in-terror-trial">acquitted of the terrorist charges laid against him</a> in a court in Punjab. The court found the prosecutors had “miserably failed” to present any reliable evidence, despite having had seven years to do so.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/25/starmer-urged-to-intervene-in-rigged-indian-prosecution-of-british-human-rights-activist">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/film/2026/may/31/the-beatles-cultural-impact-four-biopics-sam-mendes</link>
      <description><p>Fab Four are still making waves 60 years on – and upcoming Sam Mendes films are expected to turn the hype up to 11</p><p>If anyone needed a reminder of the enduring cultural clout of the Beatles, the past few weeks have provided a glut. Firstly, there’s the small matter of <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/music/2026/may/05/paul-mccartney-the-boys-of-dungeon-lane-playback-preview-fans-abbey-road">The Boys of Dungeon Lane</a>, Paul McCartney’s 20th solo album, billed as “an adventurous and limber take on guitar music” by the Guardian.</p><p>When England announced their World Cup squad, the soundtrack was Come Together, played alongside <a href="https://www.englandfootball.com/england/mens-senior-team/stories/behind-the-scenes-world-cup-26-squad-announcement#:~:text=England%20and%20The%20Beatles%20collaborate%20on%20the%20Three%20Lions'%20official,The%20Beatles'%20iconic%20Come%20Together.">a film of fashionable young people in New York</a> and a clip of a young, puckish John Lennon. The same week Stephen Colbert was played off from his final episode of the Late Show by a Paul McCartney <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zG0HFw0edXY">rendition of Hello Goodbye</a>.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/film/2026/may/31/the-beatles-cultural-impact-four-biopics-sam-mendes">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Plane landed in Wisconsin and ‘unruly passenger’ was taken into custody before flight continued to Minnesota</p><p>A <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/business/united-airlines">United Airlines</a> flight bound for <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/minneapolis">Minneapolis</a> from <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/chicago">Chicago</a> was reportedly diverted after an “unruly passenger” tried to breach the cockpit late on Friday.</p><p>The FBI and police responded to reports of a security concern with the passenger, who was detained by police at the Dane county regional airport in Madison, Wisconsin.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/business/2026/may/30/chicago-minneapolis-united-airlines-flight-diverted-attempted-cockpit-breach">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>White House releases memo describing results of Trump’s health checkup</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/may/30/trump-health-memo-released</link>
      <description><p>President in ‘excellent’ health, despite ‘lower leg swelling’ and hand bruising after fourth hospital visit in second term</p><p><a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/donaldtrump">Donald Trump</a> has been grappling with “lower leg swelling” as well as “benign” hand bruising but remains in excellent health, the US president’s physician said in a memo released by the White House.</p><p>Citing the results of a recent examination, the memo from Dr Sean Barbabella said Trump “remains in excellent health, demonstrating strong cardiac, pulmonary, neurological and overall physical function”.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/may/30/trump-health-memo-released">Continue reading...</a></description>
